query: France Ebola case 2026: current status of the doctor who tested positive after aid mission, any secondary cases, ECDC reporting. How many confirmed Ebola cases in France as of July 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings on the France Ebola case 2026:

**Key findings:**

- **The doctor's current status: RECOVERED.** On July 4, 2026, French Health Minister Stéphanie Rist announced the ALIMA humanitarian physician had recovered and was discharged from hospital after two negative PCR tests, returning home safely.

- **Total confirmed cases in France as of July 2026: ONE (1).** This was France's first-ever Ebola case detected on national territory (confirmed June 24, 2026) and the first imported case in Europe during the 2026 Bundibugyo outbreak.

- **Secondary cases: NONE.** Five fellow passengers on the doctor's June 23 Air France flight were identified as contacts and isolated (21-day monitoring), but no onward transmission occurred.

- **ECDC reporting:** On June 24, 2026, ECDC called on EU Member States to strengthen preparedness and assessed the risk of sustained transmission within the EU/EEA as "very low," provided effective detection/isolation measures are in place. It published a preparedness checklist and related technical guidance.

- **Broader context:** The outbreak (rare Bundibugyo strain, PHEIC declared May 17, 2026) had grown to 1,759 confirmed cases and 600 deaths in DRC as of July 8, 2026.

sources.title: First Ebola case confirmed in France, health ministry says
sources.snippet: France has confirmed its first case of Ebola — a doctor who had returned from a humanitarian mission in the DRC. First Ebola case confirmed in Europe.

sources.title: First Ebola patient in France 'recovered' and 'discharged from hospital'
sources.snippet: After two negative PCR tests, the patient is now recovered and has returned home. Five passengers on the Air France flight were isolated as contacts.

sources.title: Ebola response worker who traveled from DRC to France is first in outbreak to test positive outside of Africa
sources.snippet: First reported case in France and first to test positive outside of Africa amid the second large Ebola outbreak on record in DRC, driven by the rare Bundibugyo strain.

sources.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor returning from DR Congo
sources.snippet: The patient is in isolation, following strict biosafety protocols to prevent risk of spread in France.

sources.title: France Ebola patient recovers: minister
sources.snippet: The physician left the medical establishment and returned home, Health Minister Stephanie Rist said. First time France has detected Ebola.

sources.title: As first imported Ebola case is recorded in Europe, ECDC calls on EU Member States to continue investing in preparedness
sources.snippet: Risk of sustained transmission within the EU/EEA is very low provided effective measures for early detection, isolation and treatment are in place. Ebola is not airborne.

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: As of 9 July 2026, outbreak caused by Bundibugyo virus. DRC reported 1,759 confirmed cases including 600 related deaths (8 July 2026).

sources.title: Europe's First Imported Ebola Case Triggers New Travel Controls and Continent-Wide Preparedness Push
sources.snippet: Humanitarian physician (ALIMA) first confirmed case on French soil, triggering contact tracing (21-day isolation) and DRC travel restrictions. No secondary cases mentioned.

sources.title: Ebola Arrives in France: Infected Aid Doctor Flew Commercial as 1,118 DRC Cases Mount
sources.snippet: France confirmed first case June 24, 2026 — a physician with ALIMA who had been treating Ebola patients. DRC caseload ~1,118.

sources.title: Ebola virus outbreak 2026 - Public Health - European Commission
sources.snippet: WHO determined on 17 May 2026 the Ebola outbreak caused by Bundibugyo virus in DRC and Uganda constitutes a PHEIC.

data_points.metric: Negative PCR tests confirming patient recovery
DRC confirmed Ebola cases at time of France case
